bullets: - **Anthropic shipped new admin analytics and cost controls for Claude Enterprise**, adding usage-and-cost breakdowns by user and group, model-level entitlements (so routine work doesn't default to the priciest model), and spend-threshold alerts at 75% and 90%. For any TA team piloting agentic sourcing or screening on Claude, this is the governance layer that makes agent spend budgetable and stops recruiters getting blocked mid-task — set caps and model defaults before you scale a pilot. ([Claude by Anthropic](https://claude.com/blog/giving-admins-more-visibility-and-control-over-claude-usage-and-spend)) (2 July)

- **Claude — including Sonnet 5 — reached general availability in Microsoft Foundry**, running inside your Azure environment with Azure authentication, billing, governance, and a US data zone. TA leaders who must keep candidate data governed and in-region now have a compliant path to deploy Claude-based recruiting agents without standing up separate infrastructure. ([Microsoft Azure](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/blog/claude-in-microsoft-foundry-is-now-generally-available/)) (1 July)
